content.introduction: 📌 This Product Requirements Document (PRD) outlines the development of an in-app Customer Messaging Inbox. The primary goal of this project is to replace email as the main asynchronous communication channel between customers and Tamara. This inbox will unify all customer-Tamara interactions, including live chat conversations and support ticket updates, into a single, persistent experience. This initiative is part of **Phase 1** and is targeted for **Q1 2026**. This work aligns with the broader "Unified in-app messaging and AI sidekick" strategic initiative, as referenced in [https://www.notion.so/tamaracom/PRD-Unified-in-app-messaging-AI-sidekick-for-Q1-2026-2aa62429127880769e6dc4e481985a5a](https://www.notion.so/tamaracom/PRD-Unified-in-app-messaging-AI-sidekick-for-Q1-2026-2aa62429127880769e6dc4e481985a5a).
content.problem_statement: ❗ Currently, customer support interactions with Tamara are fragmented and often frustrating due to reliance on email. Customers experience several issues: * **Lost Context:** Conversations are hard to track, especially across different devices or when multiple issues arise, leading to customers repeating information. * **Slow Replies & Tracking:** The asynchronous nature of email, combined with a lack of real-time status updates, creates a perception of slow responses and opaque progress on open issues. * **Disjointed Experience:** There is no single, consistent place for customers to manage all their communications with Tamara, requiring them to switch between email and the app. * **High Repeat Contacts:** Customers often initiate new contacts for follow-ups or clarifications on existing issues because the current system doesn't provide clear progress updates or an easy way to continue a thread. This increases support volume and operational costs.
content.goals: 🎯 The primary goals for the in-app Customer Messaging Inbox are: * **Replace Email:** Establish the in-app inbox as the primary asynchronous communication channel between customers and Tamara for support and general inquiries. * **Unify Communication:** Provide a single, consistent place within the Tamara app for all customer-Tamara interactions, including support conversations and ticket updates. * **Improve Customer Experience:** Enhance transparency and ease of follow-up for customers, reducing friction in getting support. * **Reduce Support Load:** Decrease the volume of repeat contacts and follow-up emails by providing persistent context and clear status updates.
content.success_metrics: 📊 Success will be measured by our ability to shift customer behavior and improve satisfaction, aligning with our CPX 2025 strategic initiatives: * **Reduce Email Support Volume:** Decrease the percentage of support contacts initiated via email by **20%** within three months post-launch. * **Increase In-App Support Engagement:** Achieve **70%** of all asynchronous support interactions occurring via the in-app inbox within six months post-launch. * **Improve Customer Satisfaction (CSAT):** Increase CSAT scores related to support interactions by **5 points** within six months post-launch. * **Reduce Repeat Contacts:** Decrease the rate of customers initiating multiple contacts for the same issue by **15%** within six months post-launch. * **Improve Perceived Response Time:** Achieve a **10%** improvement in customer perception of response times (measured via post-interaction surveys). * **Increase Chatbot Containment:** While not directly owned by this project, this inbox will provide a clearer entry point for chatbot interactions, contributing to an overall goal of increasing chatbot containment for common queries.
content.user_personas: 👤 The target audience for this feature is **all Tamara customers who currently interact with us via email for support, inquiries, or follow-ups.** These users often: * **"The Follow-Up Frustrated":** A customer who has an ongoing issue (e.g., a refund, a payment dispute) and frequently checks their email for updates. They get frustrated when they can't easily find the latest communication or have to start a new email thread to get a status update. They value transparency and quick access to information. * **"The Multi-Tasker":** A customer who manages multiple Tamara orders or accounts. They might have several open questions or issues at once and struggle to keep track of different email threads. They need a clear way to organize and follow up on distinct topics without mixing conversations. * **"The Digital Native":** A customer who prefers in-app communication over email for most services. They expect modern apps to provide a dedicated messaging experience and find email-based support cumbersome and outdated.
content.solution: 💡 We will build a dedicated in-app Customer Messaging Inbox within the Tamara application. This inbox will serve as a single, unified hub for all customer-Tamara communications, aiming to replicate the intuitive experience of platforms like Airbnb's Messages tab. **Core components for Phase 1 include:** * **Support Inbox (Async Messaging):** Customers can initiate messages to Tamara directly from the app, receive replies asynchronously, and continue the same conversation thread over an extended period (days/weeks). * **Multi-threaded Conversations:** The inbox will support multiple distinct chat threads, allowing customers to easily manage separate topics (e.g., "Refund follow-up," "Order issue," "Account query") without losing context or mixing discussions. This will include **AI-powered thread titling** to automatically summarize conversation topics, aligning with the "Unified in-app messaging and AI sidekick" PRD. * **Ticket Updates Inside the Thread:** Whenever a support ticket is created, updated, or resolved, the customer will see these status changes as clear timeline events or messages directly within the relevant conversation thread. This project will integrate with and align with the work of another team that is building the broader messaging and notification center.
content.solution_design: 📐 The in-app Customer Messaging Inbox will feature a user interface similar to a modern chat application, accessible via a dedicated tab within the Tamara app. * **Inbox View:** * A list of active and past conversation threads, sorted by recent activity. * Each thread will display a clear title (AI-generated or user-defined), the last message, and a timestamp. * Visual indicators for unread messages. * A prominent "New Message" or "Contact Support" entry point. * **Conversation Thread View:** * A chronological display of messages between the customer and Tamara, including agent replies and automated updates. * **Async Messaging:** Customers can type and send messages at any time, and Tamara agents can reply asynchronously. The conversation history remains persistent. * **Multi-threading:** Users can initiate new threads for different topics. The system (potentially with AI assistance) will suggest creating new threads if a conversation diverges significantly from its original topic. * **Ticket Updates:** System messages will appear within the thread to denote key support ticket lifecycle events (e.g., "Ticket #12345 Created," "Your ticket has been assigned to [Agent Name]," "Awaiting information from you," "Ticket #12345 Resolved"). These messages will be distinct from agent replies. * **Cross-device Persistence:** All conversation history and thread states will be synchronized across all customer devices where the Tamara app is installed. * **Search Functionality:** A search bar will allow customers to quickly find specific messages or threads based on keywords, thread titles, or dates.
content.inclusions_and_exclusions: ✅ **Inclusions (Phase 1):** * Core in-app Support Inbox functionality for asynchronous messaging. * Ability for customers to initiate new support conversations. * Persistent, multi-threaded conversations with AI-powered thread titling. * Integration of support ticket status updates as timeline events within relevant threads. * Basic text-based messaging (sending and receiving). * Search functionality within the inbox. * Cross-device persistence for all conversation history. * Basic UI/UX for the inbox and conversation views, aligned with Tamara's existing design system and the Airbnb Messages tab reference. **Exclusions (Phase 1 - to be considered in future phases):** * Rich media support (e.g., sending images, videos, documents directly in chat, beyond what is currently supported in email attachments). * Live chat integration (while the inbox will contain live chat conversations, the *initiation* of new live chat sessions is not part of this phase). * Proactive push notifications for new messages (this falls under the broader notification center work by another team, but will be a clear integration point). * Complex routing or agent-side tooling improvements (focus is on customer-facing experience). * Advanced chatbot integration beyond entry points (e.g., full AI sidekick capabilities). * Voice or video calls. * In-app messaging for marketing or promotional purposes (focus is on support and service communication).
content.rollout_plan: 🚀 The roll-out of the in-app Customer Messaging Inbox will follow a phased approach to ensure stability, gather feedback, and manage the transition from email-centric support. 1. **Internal Testing (Month 1):** * Pilot with internal Tamara teams (e.g., Customer Support, Product, Engineering) to identify bugs and gather initial usability feedback. * Refine core functionality and user experience. 2. **Limited Beta Program (Month 2):** * Launch to a small, opted-in group of power users or loyal customers. * Collect structured feedback through surveys and direct interviews. * Monitor key metrics (e.g., engagement, CSAT for beta users) and address critical issues. 3. **Gradual Roll-out (Month 3-4):** * Progressive roll-out to a wider customer segment (e.g., 10%, 25%, 50% of the user base). * Communicate the benefits of the new inbox clearly to encourage adoption and guide users away from email for support. * Continuously monitor performance metrics and customer feedback. 4. **Full Availability & Email Deprecation (Month 5+):** * Make the in-app inbox available to all customers. * Implement strategies to gradually deprecate email as the primary support channel, guiding users towards the in-app experience (e.g., in-app prompts, auto-replies on support emails directing to the app). * Conduct post-launch reviews to assess impact on goals and identify areas for future enhancements.

metadata.project_description:
Build an in-app Customer Messaging Inbox to replace email as the primary async communication channel between customers and Tamara, and to embed live chat conversations and support ticket updates in one unified experience.

    End state UX: similar to Airbnb's Messages tab, which acts as a single inbox for conversations (including support) with clear threads, search, and a consistent place to follow up.

    Core components (Phase 1):
    - Support Inbox (async messaging): customers can message Tamara from the app, receive replies asynchronously, and continue the same thread over days/weeks (instead of email).
    - Multi-threaded conversations: Multiple chat threads (e.g., "Refund follow-up", "Order issue", "Account issue"), so customers don't lose context or mix topics.
    - Ticket updates inside the thread: whenever a support ticket is created/updated/resolved, the customer sees status updates as timeline events/messages (e.g., "Ticket created", "Assigned", "Awaiting info", "Resolved").

    Why: Email-based support is fragmented: customers lose context, replies are slow, and threads are hard to track across devices. In-app messaging gives a single, persistent place for customers to contact support, get chatbot help, track progress of open issues, and continue the conversation without starting over. This should reduce repeat contacts and improve perceived transparency.

    Target audience: all customers who currently contact Tamara via email for support follow-ups, updates, clarifications, and document sharing.

    Related work: Another team is building the messaging and notification center. This PRD should align with that work and reference it where relevant.
